Agent.description: An agent: a worker in the agent tree (Bible sec 14). The root agent has no parent; sub-agents record their parent and the host records their children, so the tree is walkable from either end.

ApprovalRequest.description: A request for the user to approve an effectful action before it runs.

Artifact.description: A produced artifact: a file, patch, report, or other output. `produced_by` links back to the plan step (Bible sec 14) that generated it.

ArtifactKind.description: The kind of artifact a step or turn produces.

Attachment.description: An attachment carried on a user message. Mirrors `hide_core::types::BlobRef` so the compat bridge maps between them losslessly.

Blocker.description: The turn cannot proceed and needs the user. Distinct from an error: a blocker is a solvable stall, not a failure.

Budget.description: The resource budget an agent may spend.

Checkpoint.description: A checkpoint: a named, restorable boundary in a thread. It may bind a state capsule (the runtime bytes) and/or a VCS ref (the source-tree position).

ClientCapabilities.description: What the client can do. The server ANDs these with its own to reach the effective capability set.

Completion.description: The turn finished. Carries the terminal status and an optional summary.

CompletionStatus.description: Terminal outcome of a turn, agent, or completion item.

ContextReceipt.description: A context receipt: the honest ledger of what was pulled into context for a turn (Bible: bounded recall is the moat, so the receipt is first-class).

ContextScope.description: What a sub-agent is allowed to see of its parent's context.

ContextSource.description: One source folded into the model's context, with the tokens it cost.

Cost.description: An estimated cost. Every field is optional so a planner can declare only what it can estimate. These are estimates, not measurements -- the honest framing this crate holds to.

Diff.description: A per-file diff with reviewable status.

Effect.description: A declared side effect. A step or agent lists the effects it may cause so a scope check can gate it before execution. Kept protocol-local (rather than reusing another crate's effect enum) so this schema authority is self-contained.

InitializeRequest.description: The client's opening message (sec 15.3): who it is, which protocol versions it speaks (highest preference first), and what it can do.

InitializeResult.description: The server's reply: who it is, the ONE negotiated protocol version, and its capabilities.

Interrupt.description: An interrupt of the running turn (sec 15 `turn/interrupt`).

Item.description: An item: one event in a turn, ordered by `seq`. The `kind` payload is flattened so an item is a single flat object on the wire.

ItemDelta.description: A streaming delta applied to an already-added item (for token-by-token agent messages and live shell output).

Method.description: The closed set of request methods (Bible sec 15), grouped by namespace. Serializes as its slash-namespaced string.

ModelPolicy.description:
The model policy an agent runs under.

DEFERRED_MODEL_REQUIRED: this only names a policy; binding a policy to an actual model and honoring `allow_fallback` at inference time is the job of a model-bearing runtime and is not implemented or claimed here.

Notification.description: Server-to-client notifications (Bible sec 15.5). Adjacently tagged with a slash-namespaced `method`, matching the request namespaces so a client routes both by the same key.

Patch.description: A proposed multi-file patch, carried as a unified diff.

PeerInfo.description: Identifies the peer at either end of the handshake.

Plan.description: A plan: a DAG of steps that reaches a goal.

PlanStep.description: One node in the plan DAG. Its edges are `dependencies`; a valid plan is acyclic and every dependency resolves to another step in the same plan (checked by [`Plan::validate_dag`]).

ReturnPolicy.description: How a sub-agent's results return to its parent.

oneOf.description: Return only a summary of what happened.
Return the full transcript of the sub-agent's turns.
Return a state capsule the parent can rebind.

Risk.description: Risk banding on an action that may need approval. Mirrors the concept in `hide_core::types::RiskLevel` but is defined locally so the schema authority stays self-contained.

RollbackBoundary.description: The rollback boundary a step commits to before mutating anything.

reference.description: A reference to the boundary marker (checkpoint id, capsule id, stash ref), when the kind implies one.

RollbackKind.description: How far a step's effects can be unwound if it is rejected or fails.

oneOf.description: Undo by reloading a state capsule.
Undo by dropping a VCS stash.

Scope.description: The blast radius a step is allowed to touch. `paths` are the files or globs it may read/write; `network` says whether it may reach out.

Session.description: A session: one working context over a workspace. Threads (including forks) hang off it; the session references them by id so a session stays a light header while threads carry the turn history.

ShellStream.description: A chunk of a running command's output stream.

StateCapsuleRef.description:
A pointer to a state capsule held by `hide-state`. This is only a reference: it names the capsule and pins its digest and identity binding so a reader can locate and verify the bytes.

DEFERRED_MODEL_REQUIRED: the live production of capsule bytes from a running engine, and their rebind into a runtime, is not implemented here and cannot be claimed. `hide-state` defines the byte schema; a model-bearing runtime fills it. This struct only carries the address and the integrity pins.

capsule_id.description: The capsule id as known to the `hide-state` store.

digest.description: Integrity digest of the capsule payload (verified by `hide-state`).

Steer.description: A mid-turn steering directive from the user (sec 15 `turn/steer`).

Thread.description: A thread: an ordered line of turns within a session. Forking a thread (sec 15 `thread/fork`, `thread/fork_ephemeral`) records the parent and the turn it branched from, so lineage is always recoverable. An ephemeral fork is a scratch branch whose only durable return is a merge summary (`thread/merge_summary`).

Turn.description: A turn: one authored contribution to a thread, carrying an ordered list of items. `parent_turn` records causal provenance (the "why" spine).

TurnStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a turn. `steer`/`interrupt`/`pause`/`resume` (sec 15) move a turn between these.

VerificationReceipt.description: The receipt returned when an oracle finishes grading.

VerificationRequest.description: A request to grade a target against an oracle.
